title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries, 2024

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

The map displays the global distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, using a binary color scheme where teal indicates eligibility and beige indicates ineligibility. Eligibility is determined by a Gross National Income per capita threshold of less than US$1,810 over the preceding three years. The teal shading covers a contiguous band across sub-Saharan Africa, including nations such as Nigeria, Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Kenya, alongside island nations like Madagascar. In Asia, the eligible category includes Pakistan, Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Myanmar, and Yemen. The map also highlights eligibility for specific countries in the Middle East and Central Asia, such as Syria and Tajikistan, as well as Papua New Guinea in Oceania and Haiti in the Caribbean. Conversely, the beige color dominates North America, Europe, Australia, and most of South America, marking these regions as not eligible for this specific financial and expert knowledge support. The visualization shows a clear geographic concentration of supported nations in the lower-income regions of Africa and parts of Asia, while higher-income economies across the Americas and Eurasia fall outside the support criteria.
